title: The Ghosts of Toni Morrison
description: In her novels, she located the missing stories of Black America.
hook: Morrison's novels recover Black America's missing stories—a cultural criticism revealing psychology beneath historical erasure.

title: Issey Miyake
description:
‘The only thing I retained from Grose’s lecture about Lacanian psychoanalysis was a comment about clothes.’ 

Akshi Singh on clothes, fantasy and Issey Miyake.

The post Issey Miyake appeared first on Granta.
hook: How Lacanian psychoanalysis meets fashion: exploring clothes, fantasy, and designer Issey Miyake's sensory methodology.

title: Reading at Random with Virginia Woolf
description: In Woolf’s final, unfinished manuscript, she employs a “methodology of disorder” that enables “that state of mind in which it seems possible to us to write the book, not to read it.”
hook: Virginia Woolf's unfinished manuscript employs disorder as method—contemplative writing rewarding slowness and intellectual rigor.

title: The Disappearing Act by Maria Stepanova review – a poetic exploration of Russian guilt
description:
Written from exile after the full-scale invasion of Ukraine, this autofictional blend of memory and fable tracks a changing sense of self

M, a 50-year-old novelist living in an idyllic place by a lake, is travelling to a literary festival to give a talk. A sequence of events, mostly beyond her control, leaves her stranded in an unfamiliar town. It’s dead quiet, except for a travelling circus camped on the outskirts. M checks into a hotel, ignores her phone and wanders around,
hook: Autofictional exile narrative blending memory and fable—exploring shifting identity and Russian complicity through poetic interiority.

title: A photojournalist details her rebellion against the Syrian regime -- and her father
description: Loubna Mrie grew up in Syria, where her father was allegedly an assassin for the regime. She joined the Syrian revolution first as a protester and then as a photojournalist. Her memoir is Defiance.
hook: Photojournalist's memoir documents rebellion against Syrian regime and father—intimate reckoning with family, regime violence, and reinvention.
